_doc: JobPilot policy template. Copy to data/policy.json (gitignored) and edit: `cp docs/policy.example.json data/policy.json`. Every section is optional — anything you leave out falls back to the neutral defaults (nothing refused, nothing deprioritized, location gate off). Keys starting with '_' are documentation only and are ignored by the loader. All matching is case-insensitive substring matching unless noted.

scoring._doc: Consumed by the pre-apply fit scorer (core/job_scorer.py).

refused_companies._doc: Companies you refuse to apply to, as a {name: reason} map. A match hard-zeros the fit score and the recommendation becomes 'REFUSED by policy'. The reason shows up in the score's risk list; use "" to omit it. A plain list of names also works.

refused_title_keywords._doc: Title substrings that hard-zero the fit score regardless of company.
refused_title_keywords.example keyword: why roles like this are refused

deprioritized_companies._doc: Companies that get a -25 score penalty and a downgraded recommendation instead of a hard refusal (e.g. places where applying has never converted for you).

queue._doc: Consumed by the queue builder / Lane-C scorer (core/queue_builder.py).
queue._doc_title_kill_keywords: Title substrings that hard-gate a job to score 5 so it never ranks. Typical use: management-ladder titles you never want queued.
queue._doc_moat_company_tags: {company: industry-tag} map, assigned at scan time because JD text is not fetched. Tags listed in high_moat_industries earn full vertical-moat points (25); other tagged industries earn 15; untagged companies earn the neutral 6.
queue._doc_high_moat_industries: Industry tags (from moat_company_tags) where you have deep lived experience — your vertical moat.
queue._doc_excluded_industries: Industry tags you want softly excluded: their logistics sub-score is slashed to 2 instead of being hard-gated.
queue._doc_company_hq: Optional {company: city} additions/overrides to the built-in factual company-to-HQ table, used as a location fallback when a posting carries no location.

location_gate._doc: Hard location gate. Disabled by default — every location passes. When enabled, a job qualifies only if its LOCATION fields (role location, falling back to company HQ) match allowed_locations, remote_terms, or country_terms. The job title can only disqualify (via blocked terms), never qualify — so a title like 'Help us build…' cannot pass a US gate through the word 'us'. All terms are word-anchored: 'us' matches 'Austin, US' but not 'status'.
location_gate._doc_blocked_locations: Hard blocks, checked against both the location fields and the title.
location_gate._doc_blocked_without_country: Blocked unless a country term also appears in the location — e.g. 'canada' blocks 'Remote Canada' but allows 'Remote US/Canada'.
